query: PlugShare EV charging app review demo tutorial

transcripts.title: EV Mini series, Plugshare
transcripts.transcript:
hey how's it going everyone today I'm
going to run you through an important
phone app that you should have on your
phone if you drive an EV of some sort my
name's salon and this is juice garage
now we've all been there it's just been
one of those days where you've got a
million things to do but nothing's gone
quite to plan you hop in the car only to
realize you're low on fuel and in an
area you're not familiar with now in
this situation you probably pull your
phone out and have a Google but what
happens if I told you there's an even
better way to search for it and that way
is through the app called plug share
plugshare it's basically a map that
What is Plugshare?
allows you to find all the public
charging stations around where you're
located now the great thing about this
app is that it's completely free it
works worldwide in most countries and
it's available on iOS Android and most
web platforms now once you've installed
Get Started
the app go ahead and open it up and
follow the prompts to create an account
now we highly recommend that you do
create an account because it'll help
open up the extra features of the app
Keys and Legends
once you've done that you'll be greeted
by the maps page now what is the maps
page well it's basically a map showing
your location and all the surrounding
public charging stations now in this
case my location is denoted by the blue
circle if you can't see a blue circle
for your location it's probably because
the GPS or location services on your
phone is currently turned off so go
ahead and turn that on and you should be
able to see where you are as you can see
there's a bunch of green and orange
icons here now the main difference
between the two is that anything noted
in Orange is basically a fast charging
or one of the more powerful charging
stations anything noted in green is just
a general public charger
you can also see that there are icons
with a spanner located on them now
that's to indicate that that charging
station is currently going undergoing
construction maybe some form of
Maintenance or it's just not working so
Charging Station Profile
now let's have a look into one of these
charging stations so I'm just going to
zoom into the one here in zetland and
tap on that and it's going to give me a
pop-up Banner for the charging station
what we can see is that there's a title
for the charging station in this case
it's the Super Center Mall Park charging
station and you can see a number on the
left hand side now that number is called
the plug score
what that is is basically as people
attend that charging station they leave
a review for the charging station and
then that's the aggregate score off that
in this case they've got a 7.9 out of
10.
so if we click onto the banner it'll
actually take us into the profile for
the public charging station
so
as you can see we're greeted by the
Super Center Moore Park charging station
it's got a score of 7.9
there is a Tesla charger here and it's
about 3.39 kilometers or a 10 minute
drive from where I am now up the top
we've got photos now these photos have
been taken by other users of plug share
and uploaded so they can be things like
car park hours or where the charging
station is located or even if someone's
parked there who shouldn't have had just
Check-in function
below that we can see the check in icon
now what is the check-in icon well if
you've got an account with plug share
you can tap on that to say that you're
currently at that charging station and
you're using it the benefit of this is
that it allows other users to interact
with each other so for example let's say
I was at this charging station and I
left my card there to charge and I was
off shopping someone rocks up and they
realize that oh the car is fully charged
they can actually message me through the
app to say hey your car is fully charged
can you please come move it so I can
park my car below that we've also got
the details so this is the actual
address for the public charging station
as well as a contact number so if
something is wrong with the public
charging station you can actually call
them up and get that resolved
now on the bottom right you can see that
Get Direction
there's a get directions icon that's
basically going to connect to whatever
Maps app you have on your phone in this
case we've got Apple Maps and Google
Maps so I can choose from one of the two
and it'll give me directions directly
from where I am to the charging station
and so below that we've got information
about the charging station itself so if
I tap onto that you can see that there's
a description for it so information like
that is really useful to know
below that we've got amenities so what's
available at the charging location so in
this case it is a shopping center so
they would have retail stores as well as
dining amenities toilets things like
that
and then you can see the plug type so if
you're not familiar with the terminology
of plug types but you do recognize it
visually they've got that indicator on
the screen as well to help you out and
then underneath that we've got the
check-in so as I mentioned earlier
people who Rock up to these charging
stations they basically leave a review
on the charging station and in this case
for the most part they've got mostly
green ticks there's one noted up here in
red saying the hardware was broken when
they arrived there was no power to the
Tesla charger and that was done on the
29th of April and then down below we've
got the photo gallery which is connected
to the photos that I showed you at the
Filter Function
start so you might be looking at the
screen now and be thinking there's a lot
of charging stations here how can I
simplify this for myself well you can
actually set it up so it only shows you
charging stations which are suitable for
your car now the way you do that is you
go into the filters section and in this
case for the purpose of the
demonstration I have all of the charging
stations ticked on and what I'm going to
Select Vehicle
do is I'm going to select switch vehicle
and so what I can do here is I can go to
manage vehicles and I can add in a
vehicle so in this case let's do
something like a pulse star 2 and add
vehicle so now what the app is doing is
basically going through its database and
saying all right where's pole star twos
have so and so connection and this is
what we're going to filter out for you
instead so when I tap done
if the screen will refresh and now I'm
just looking at charging stations
applicable to my vehicle
Plug Type Selection Filter
now if I go back into the filter section
you can actually see that there's a
bunch of incompatible plugs so for
example if there's one that you might

turn that on as well
below that you can also see that there's
public locations home locations and
in-use locations which are the ones
showed in Gray and then you can also
filter out even further for particular
charging rate so if you want a 50
kilowatt 70 kilowatt 80 kilowatt you
only want those ones to pop up you can
also set up for that or you can even set
up the minimum plugs plug score so if
you don't want to go to charging
stations with a rating of five and below
you can set that up as well it's
completely up to you so now let's move
Trips Function
on to the trips tab so what is the trips
tab well let's say you're going on a
long journey from let's say Sydney up to
Brisbane and you don't know which
charging stations you need to stop at or
how far you can get on the charge that
you have well plugshare actually does
all those calculations for you so what
I'm going to do is I'm going to create a
trip as an example I'm going to use the
pole star 2 as my vehicle I'm going to
say from my current location let's go
all the way up to Brisbane now if you're
not driving the car that's showing on
the screen you can just tap on change
your vehicle and select the one that you
are but for this case I'm just going to
keep with the pole star 2 and down below
that you can see your vehicle range now
this is basically part of plug shares
database saying that okay the average
pole star will probably get 350 odd
kilometers now if you find that your car
isn't getting that range you can
actually reduce the range of your
vehicle so in this case to be on the
safer side I'm going to go with 300
kilometers instead
and then below that it's asking your
starting charge now that's basically
saying at the moment you're going to set
off on this trip what is the range left
on your vehicle so for me I'm going to
just say that I've got a hundred K's
left
and now once I hit find root it's
basically going to do some calculations
based off those inputs and here you can
see that I've got a couple options to
get up to Brisbane from Sydney so what
I'm going to do is I'm just going to
take the first option using the Pacific
Highway and now it's showing me all the
compatible charges so before I said I've
only got 100 kilometers worth of range
right now
and is basically saying okay these are
the charging stations available and with
100ks of range that's how far you can
get so let's say I charge up at the one
in Gosford I'm going to tap on that and
I'm going to press add to trip and now
it's going to assume that I'm going to
go there get a full charge and I'm going
to set off again so now it's
recalculated to say all right now you've
got about 300 K's worth of charge how
far can I get so let's say I go up to
Port Macquarie I tap on that charger I
want to get a full charge there I'm
going to hit add to trip and then it's
going to recalculate again for the next
set so I'm going to charge up at Coffs
Harbor add to trip and so on and so
forth now
as you can see when you get up to the
Gold Coast there's actually a charger
that's inactive now again really
important information to know because
let's say you did this on Google it may
not say that that charging station isn't
working at the moment so you might get
up there you might have only a couple
K's left of charge and realize like
shoot I can't actually charge up here
I'm gonna have to find an alternative so
with this app it actually really helps
you plan out your trip in its entirety
so just to finish this off I'm going to
add another trip I'm going to hit save
and now it's calculated my entire route
and I can go to the view list at the
bottom here so it's saying a quick
summary saying you're going to go to
these charging stations and that's the
rough distance between each charging
station
so bookmarks now bookmarks is basically
Bookmark
listing all your favorite locations so
whichever charging station you prefer to
go to you can just save it in lock it
into place so if let's say you're not
the one driving the car and one of your
mates or someone from your family is and
you prefer them to use a particular
charging station you can just send them
the location using your bookmarks so now
Account Profile Setting
we'll have a look at the profile section
so this is basically my account for
juice garage and it shows my email
address my name and the car that I'm
driving again the poster too
now we've got a messages tab so let's
say someone was to reach out to you
using the check-in function at one of
the charging stations you can find that
information in this section then you've
got the trip planner any alerts or
important alerts for maybe charging
stations you've got bookmarked you can
find them there
you've got your charge history so let's
say you had to pay for a charging
station this will have your history of
payments on there as well
and then your recent activity if you
want to add a home charger
things like that so that was a quick
once over of some of the key features
you can find on the plug share app now
if you have any questions just drop them
down in the comment section below and
we'll do our best to answer them we are
Mini Series Overview
planning to make more videos like this
about some of the other apps in the EV
industry so if you don't see one
mentioned on the screen in front of you
go ahead and drop that in the comment

thank you for watching and we'll see you

transcripts.title: Plugshare Intro: Plugshare Trip planner
transcripts.transcript:
today i'm going to go through the plug share app both mobile and desktop as well as cover the trip planner and how you can use it on
your smartphone
back to the channel and thanks for being here i've mentioned the plugshare app before and i've recommended it to people both current ev drivers as well as
people looking to get into their first electric vehicle i personally think it is one of the best apps out there for public charging stations because it
shows more charging stations on a variety of networks and not just one network find the plug share app a lot more
comprehensive it's for this reason that i recommend anybody who's looking at buying their first electric vehicle download the app open it up and zoom in
on your area you'll be amazed and probably surprised at how many public charging stations are available in your immediate area so today i'm
going to walk through both the desktop version and the mobile version because they do look slightly different and in particular on the desktop version
only you get access to a trip planner which is really cool for people that have limited range or are planning long road trips
i will show you exactly how you can actually plan your trip on the desktop and then use the mapping and the waypoints on your mobile phone
if you're not new to plug share and you're only here because you want to see how you can use the trip planner on your mobile device i'll put a time code right here that you
can fast forward to and go directly to that part in the video so let's start i'm going to start with the desktop app because that's what i have logged in here so i'm going to
turn to my computer here and i'm going to show you exactly how i set up my plug share for optimal use for me and then you can play with the app and
see what works for you the best so to access the desktop version just open up your web browser and type in plugshare.com it's important to note you
actually don't need to register for an account however by doing so you get a few extra features and i'll go through those
in particular you cannot use the trip planner and you cannot log into stations and leave notes if you do not have an account so the first
thing i would actually do when you set up your account is i would actually just go in and manage your vehicles and then add the vehicle that you have so i added
my nissan leaf and i have a nissan leaf sl so i do have quick charging abilities i'm gonna add in a chevy because i noticed this
earlier so if you type in chevy and a bolt ev regardless of what trim because i can't actually pick the trim on this i can
pick the color but regardless of the trim it tells me that there's a type 2 and a ccs
combo fast charger plug now i know for a fact that not every trim level of a bolt has the ccs plugs so
if you're unsure make sure before you register that in now you can still add the vehicle and it will log in here but you're going to
want to turn off this ccs plug if your vehicle does not have that so i would just confirm that the compatible plugs that are showing up
are the ones that your car actually has so the first thing you can do is you can open up the legend so you figure out exactly what each pin means
the green ones are your public ones and the orange ones are your high power or also known as your level three charging stations
if you don't understand the difference between level one two and three charging i'll put a link to a video that i did previously that'll go over all of that
just covering all the bases here we got the legend we got some filters that we can set up and i'm gonna go over the filters in depth a little bit more
you can add a station so if you don't see a station on here but you know it exists you can help out the eevee community and add it
in here and it'll drop a pin on the map so the first thing i'm going to do is i'm going to start filtering out these stations so that i actually
get less stations on the map but they're more likely to work for my particular interest now you can filter by a lot of different settings however some of them
i would say are in beta mode because they don't work 100 the way that they're expected to so the first thing i do
the plugs so i set this up to show all the different available stations including ones with plugs that don't work for me i don't drive a tesla so none of these
teslas would work if i just reset to the plugs that my vehicle has i get the chatimo and i get the standard j1772
the minimum power i don't really care personally if i'm looking for a charge it's because i'm in need yes i would like it to be as fast as
possible however any of the public charging stations that are fast chargers are going to be just fine for my vehicle because it is a little bit older
and doesn't have that new charging availability up to that 100 kilowatt or 100 kilowatt plus charging rates
so i don't filter by network even though in my area there's only about three or four networks that actually exist right now however i leave it unfiltered because
new stations are popping up every day and i don't want to miss one that might be under a different network but more convenient to me just because i filtered
it out so i leave all the networks on here is one of the areas that i would say is somewhat in beta mode because the
payment required locations in particular does not work 100 and i will show you exactly what i mean
you'll notice there's a cluster up here and there's a couple down here with the payment required now if i turn that off you'll notice that those clusters that
cluster got a little bit smaller and the ones down here didn't show up at all the reason being is that it thinks that
those ones are pay stations however if i turn on payment required and i look at say
sungod recreational center i click on that and it'll say that payment is required but the session
cost is zero dollars meaning it actually doesn't cost anything that's one of those things where if that filter was a little bit more
polished it would know that a zero dollar charge is the same as free and it would show it whether that filter was put on or not so that's one example so i
leave the payment on so that i don't miss stations like that because you can always click you can get the details of the station
and you can determine whether or not you want to go there are definitely ones that have payments uh required this one says a
payment's required and it will give you the details down here beginning january 18th there's a fee of one dollar an hour for charging
not bad still reasonable still cheaper than gas that's about it i don't do any of the other extras
now there are ones where if i'm just in a rush i only want to see the fast chargers that's where you can turn these plugs on
and off and you can see exactly what's going gonna work for you that turned off most of the chargers but if you're looking for a fast charge
because you're in a rush that's more important you can turn that off so i will say that apps are constantly in development and new features are
coming out all the time so if you do happen to find this video in the future check the links in the description below and at the end of this video for
possible updates to what i've already just covered so i'm going to run through the trip planner here and show you a couple of the features in particular that i like about it because i've added
in the fact that i have a nissan leaf that is also going to help me with my trip planner i'm going to go into the trip planner and plan a trip
from vancouver to calgary now the first thing you can see is a green circle and you can see a whole bunch of charging stations
so this is every charging station along that route within a particular radius if you want to go off the highway a little bit more
you can up that radius right here so let's say you want to go three it's gonna add a couple charging stations on the highway now let's say you want to
go 10 that's giving you a different amount of of charging stations it doesn't look that different all zoomed out but i can assure you that as you
zoom in there's going to be more and more available in particular right here you see this is off the highway but if i dial this back
down to 1.6 a couple of them disappear this green circle is your estimated range of your vehicle i find
the cold impacts it i find the usage of the heater impacts it depending on when you're going to do your road trip you can adjust your range
accordingly right now because i have a nissan leaf the plug share app is telling me that it expects to get
a maximum range of 114 kilometers i personally don't think that's doable on the highway
and i wouldn't want to show up to a charging station totally out of juice so i would dial that back i might even give it a safe buffer and go
say to 90. let's say i'm starting on this purple dot because that's where it says i am right now the green circle says i can only get as far as
these charging stations here and then i'm going to want to fill up so there's quite a few there so you could aim for one of these and you could click
on let's say that one and add that to the trip once you have added that one to the trip first of all that white box will stay there but now
you see the green box or the green diameter has extended and says you can get this far now that's right on the edge
i may not want to aim for that one but maybe i do because i've given a little bit of a buffer so let's just say for argument's sake i want to add this one
i can click that and add to trip again now the green diameter the radius shows you exactly where you can get again
and then you keep doing that for the entire trip so these are all the stops and it'll give you the estimated drive time in between each one it's going to
give you the ability to add an estimated wait time if you scroll all the way to the bottom of your trip it will tell you the total distance and
driving time that is your driving time 10 hours and 45 minutes but if you include the time with waiting
it's 11 hours and 45 minutes so it's actually not waiting it is charging time they should reword that in my opinion so
let's say you had to charge for 30 minutes and you waited for 30 minutes i would just add an hour to each of these as i've added them
now the total time with charging goes up to 15 hours or almost 16 hours if you did it all the way through i've got three four more stops
it would probably end up closer to 20 hours so it does add a fair bit of time on my particular car because i have such
a low range with just the 24 kilowatt hour battery personally would not do this road trip in this car just because i wouldn't want to double
the amount of time it took to get to somewhere but you could see how this is useful and if you had a newer vehicle with a longer
range you wouldn't have to stop as much and you could plan this out the other really important thing and the thing i love about the trip planner app
the most is that you can check the elevation of your drive because we all know that driving uphill
you're gonna get less mileage out of your charge and going downhill you will get more this one here i would be
very cautious about reaching this charger if it was at the max limit of that green radius that it was shown
on the previous map i'd be less concerned about getting to this one and i may not even need to get to this
one because i'm downhill i might be able to skip number five and drive all the way to number six if that elevation declined what got me
enough charge on the regenerative braking or the regenerative charging in order to get me further so you can have a look at this you may have to add
in more charging stops or fewer depending on the elevation that you are driving so like i said at the beginning you do
need the desktop app in order to do the trip planner it's not available on the mobile app which is a little bit of a failure on the apps part however i would
say that the plug share app does display better on a desktop and for trip planning purposes it's going to be a bit more beneficial to
have the big screen so you can see the radius you can check the elevation and you can check where all the different charging stations
are on your route now that said when you're driving you are probably using the gps on your phone
in order to follow directions at least that's how i operate so i'm going to show you how you can plan your trip on the desktop
and use the waypoints and the route that you've planned on your mobile app as you go so i have
my route planned to calgary vancouver to calgary and all my waypoints are here those are my stops i've got in all my settings
it is what it is now we're ready to rock and roll once you have your trip planned here you scroll down here to click view directions
and the only thing you have to do here is you say send directions to your phone it's going to come up with a bunch of different options i just email it to myself it comes up with
a link i'll show you that on my mobile device here and as you can see i click on this link the map is
available it tells me the time estimated to the next or the first waypoint and it does that for the entire duration
of the trip so the mobile app doesn't look a ton different from the desktop app it's really just a little bit more condensed and it's got a lot of the same
features as the desktop app it just does not have the trip planner so i'll just run through a couple of them right here i'll just
still got the pin points on the map the menu is a little bit smaller it's just in this corner here and then it takes up more of the screen
as you're looking at it however the filters are in a slightly different place so the filters are in the top right
corner right here and as you can see they don't look like tile graphics the same way they do on the desktop
they're more just toggle switches so and it will take the settings from your desktop app if you registered for an account that's
another benefit for actually logging in and having an account it actually saves those settings for you both desktop and mobile and then
connectors so you can see that i've got my nissan leaf at the top if i wanted to change vehicle my bolt is in there from adding it
previously the nema 1450 may be useful for me in the future because the level 2 charger that i installed in my garage is somewhat
portable and has a 1450 plug on it so if i was road tripping i might want to turn that on and see if there was maybe
an rv park or a service station that maybe had one of those plugs available to me that wasn't going to be in use 99 of the
time so this little button in the bottom right hand corner is searching along a route it's not the same as the trip planner however if
you're out and about and you're driving for a little bit during the day you may find this useful so let's say i'm gonna punch in calgary again just so
we can see exactly but it really it won't give you the ability to add waypoints but you can search for the charging stations along a
particular route that in a nutshell is the plug share app if you enjoyed the video or at the very least learn something
let me know in the comment section below and don't forget to hit the like button if you haven't already please consider hitting the subscribe button so you
don't miss out on future videos thanks again for watching and have yourself a great day
